album.name: album
album.description: The album table captures information about music albums released by various artists. This table is a critical component in managing the vast collection of music data, enabling efficient querying and analysis to support business decisions.

columns.name: AlbumId
columns.description: A unique identifier assigned to each album, serving as a primary key for fast lookup and referencing. This column enables the creation of relationships with other tables, such as artist and track.

columns.name: Title
columns.description: The official title of the album, providing a concise description of the music release. The use of VARCHAR(160) allows for a reasonable length of character strings to accommodate various album titles.

columns.name: ArtistId
columns.description: A foreign key referencing the artist table, establishing a connection between albums and their respective creators. This column enables the retrieval of artist-related information for each album.

artist.name: artist
artist.description: The Artist table is a fundamental entity in the music industry domain. It stores information about individual artists, providing a centralized repository for tracking and analyzing artist-related data.

columns.name: ArtistId
columns.description: A unique identifier assigned to each artist, serving as the primary key. This column enables efficient lookup and retrieval of artist records.

columns.name: Name
columns.description: The official name of the artist, which may include their stage name or pseudonym. This column provides a human-readable representation of the artist's identity.

customer.name: customer
customer.description: Customer information table containing details about individuals or organizations that have a relationship with the organization.

columns.name: CustomerId
columns.description: Unique identifier for each customer.

columns.name: FirstName
columns.description: The first name of the customer.

columns.name: LastName
columns.description: The last name or surname of the customer.

columns.name: Company
columns.description: The company or organization that the customer represents.

columns.name: Address
columns.description: The physical address of the customer's location.

columns.name: City
columns.description: The city where the customer is located.

columns.name: State
columns.description: The state or province where the customer is located.

columns.name: Country
columns.description: The country where the customer is located.

columns.name: PostalCode
columns.description: A unique code used for postal services to deliver mail and packages.

columns.name: Phone
columns.description: The phone number of the customer.

columns.name: Fax
columns.description: The fax number of the customer.

columns.name: Email
columns.description: The email address of the customer.

columns.name: SupportRepId
columns.description: The unique identifier for the support representative assigned to this customer.

employee.name: employee
employee.description: This table represents a collection of employee records, containing information about their personal details, job roles, and reporting structures.

columns.name: EmployeeId
columns.description: Unique identifier assigned to each employee record.

columns.name: LastName
columns.description: The employee's last name or surname.

columns.name: FirstName
columns.description: The employee's first name or given name.

columns.name: Title
columns.description: The employee's job title or role within the organization.

columns.name: ReportsTo
columns.description: The ID of the employee's direct supervisor or manager.

columns.name: BirthDate
columns.description: The date of birth for each employee.

columns.name: HireDate
columns.description: The date when the employee was hired or started working.

columns.name: Address
columns.description: The physical address of the employee's residence or workplace.

columns.name: City
columns.description: The city where the employee resides or works.

columns.name: State
columns.description: The state or province where the employee resides or works.

columns.name: Country
columns.description: The country where the employee resides or works.

columns.name: PostalCode
columns.description: The postal code or zip code for the employee's address.

columns.name: Phone
columns.description: The phone number of the employee, including area code and extension.

columns.name: Fax
columns.description: The fax number of the employee, including area code and extension.

columns.name: Email
columns.description: The email address of the employee.

genre.name: genre
genre.description: The Genre table is a fundamental component of the Music Library system, serving as a centralized repository for categorizing music genres. This table enables efficient management and retrieval of genre-related information.

columns.name: GenreId
columns.description: A unique identifier assigned to each genre, ensuring data integrity and facilitating fast lookup and retrieval operations.

columns.name: Name
columns.description: The descriptive name of the genre, providing a human-readable representation for users. The use of VARCHAR(120) allows for flexible naming conventions, accommodating various genre names and descriptions.

invoice.name: invoice
invoice.description: The invoice table captures financial transactions between a company and its customers. It provides information about the date, amount, and billing details for each transaction.
invoice.synonyms: financial transaction, sales receipt, payment record

columns.name: InvoiceId
columns.description: Unique identifier assigned to each invoice, serving as a primary key.

columns.name: CustomerId
columns.description: Foreign key referencing the customer table, linking an invoice to its corresponding customer.

columns.name: InvoiceDate
columns.description: The date when the invoice was issued or is expected to be paid.

columns.name: BillingAddress
columns.description: The physical address where the customer's billing information should be sent.

columns.name: BillingCity
columns.description: The city where the customer is located and where the invoice should be billed to.

columns.name: BillingState
columns.description: The state or province where the customer is located and where the invoice should be billed to.

columns.name: BillingCountry
columns.description: The country where the customer is located and where the invoice should be billed to.

columns.name: BillingPostalCode
columns.description: The postal code or zip code for the customer's billing address.

columns.name: Total
columns.description: The total amount of the invoice, representing the sum of all items and taxes.

invoiceline.name: invoiceline
invoiceline.description: This table represents a collection of individual line items that comprise an invoice. Each line item is associated with a specific track, and its corresponding unit price and quantity.
invoiceline.synonyms: invoice lines, order items, transactional records

columns.name: InvoiceLineId
columns.description: A unique identifier assigned to each invoice line item.

columns.name: InvoiceId
columns.description: The foreign key referencing the parent Invoice table, identifying the invoice to which this line item belongs.

columns.name: TrackId
columns.description: The foreign key referencing the Track table, specifying the track associated with this line item.

columns.name: UnitPrice
columns.description: The price per unit of the track or product being invoiced.
columns.synonyms: price per unit, cost per item

columns.name: Quantity
columns.description: The number of units of the track or product being invoiced.

mediatype.name: mediatype
mediatype.description: The Media Type table stores information about different media formats used in digital content. This table provides a centralized repository for categorizing various media types, enabling efficient management and retrieval of related data.
mediatype.synonyms: media type categories, file formats, content types

columns.name: MediaTypeId
columns.description: A unique identifier assigned to each media type, serving as the primary key for this table. This column enables fast lookup and retrieval of specific media types.
columns.synonyms: media type ID, file format code

columns.name: Name
columns.description: The human-readable name or description of a media type, providing context and clarity when referencing the corresponding MediaTypeId. This column supports flexible querying and reporting on media type characteristics.
columns.synonyms: file format name, content type label

playlist.name: playlist
playlist.description: The Playlist table stores information about various music playlists. It is used to categorize and manage a collection of songs, albums, or artists.
playlist.synonyms: music playlist, song list, album selection, artist compilation

columns.name: PlaylistId
columns.description: A unique identifier for each playlist, used to distinguish it from other playlists. This column serves as the primary key of the table.
columns.synonyms: playlist code, music ID, collection number

columns.name: Name
columns.description: The title or name given to a playlist, providing a descriptive label for the collection of songs, albums, or artists. This column allows for easy searching and filtering by playlist title.
columns.synonyms: playlist title, music list name, album selection name

playlisttrack.name: playlisttrack
playlisttrack.description: The playlisttrack table maintains a record of individual tracks that are part of specific playlists. This table enables efficient querying and analysis of track-to-playlist relationships, facilitating data-driven insights for music streaming services.
playlisttrack.synonyms: music library, track assignments, playlist compositions

columns.name: PlaylistId
columns.description: A unique identifier for a playlist, referencing the corresponding entry in the playlist table. This column serves as a foreign key to establish relationships between tracks and their respective playlists.

columns.name: TrackId
columns.description: A unique identifier for a track, referencing the corresponding entry in the track table. This column serves as a foreign key to establish relationships between tracks and their respective entries in the track table.

track.name: track
track.description: The track table contains information about individual audio tracks in a music library. It is used to store metadata, such as names, genres, and composers, along with technical details like milliseconds and bytes.

columns.name: TrackId
columns.description: A unique identifier for each track in the database. This column serves as the primary key.

columns.name: Name
columns.description: The title of the track, which is a human-readable name that describes the composition.
columns.synonyms: song title, track title, composition name

columns.name: AlbumId
columns.description: A foreign key referencing the album table. This column establishes a relationship between tracks and their respective albums.

columns.name: MediaTypeId
columns.description: A foreign key referencing the media type table. This column categorizes each track as a specific type of audio content (e.g., MP3, WAV).
columns.synonyms: media type ID, audio format ID

columns.name: GenreId
columns.description: A foreign key referencing the genre table. This column classifies each track into a particular music genre.

columns.name: Composer
columns.description: The individual or group responsible for creating the composition. This column can be null if the composer is unknown or not specified.

columns.name: Milliseconds
columns.description: A technical detail indicating the duration of each track in milliseconds.

columns.name: Bytes
columns.description: The size of each track file in bytes. This column provides information about the storage requirements for each track.

columns.name: UnitPrice
columns.description: A price value representing the cost or value of each track. This column can be used to manage pricing and revenue for digital music sales or streaming services.
columns.synonyms: track price, song price, composition price
